evidence_quotes.quote: not to mention digging down the reality of the consumer not actually owning a digital copy of the game but rather has only revokable access to the product in a bunch of fine print.

paragraphs.text: Can we please also do something about protecting consumers of digital copies of video games such as myself? Buying products digitally like video games in the current market. It's more like buying access to a product. So I don't actually own a copy of a product that I buy. I just have a "license" to use the product which for some company(ies) out there that right of access to said product can be revoked of any reason they deem is right. I as consumer can lose access to the product I bought access for whatever reason a company decides is fair. Including to shutting down games with online access only features without proper notice to me the consumer at the time of purchasing "access" to the product. I want to own a copy of a product that I buy. The world is becoming more and more digitalized I do not find it fair to me or many other consumers that we have to put up spending money on a product only to get some sort of circus ticket access that can be revoked whenever the party that sold the digital access ticket decides is okay for them. Not to mention digging down the reality of the consumer not actually owning a digital copy of the game but rather has only revokable access to the product in a bunch of fine print. Please include the sale of digital goods like video games in the Digital Fairness Act to make the transaction between seller & buyer more fair. I support the European Citizens' Initiative "Stop Destroying Videogames".
